title: The Grammar of Logic: Nominal vs. Structural
overview: Is a type defined by its name (Nominal) or by its shape (Structural)?
historical_context: Nominal typing is traditional in Java/C++; Structural typing is the "Duck Typing" of the static world (TypeScript/Go).
mental_model: Nominal: "You are a Bird because your ID card says Bird." Structural: "If you walk like a duck and quack like a duck, you are a duck."
tradeoffs: Nominal: Clearer intent, less accidental compatibility. Structural: Higher flexibility, potentially ambiguous.
legacy: Found in Go, TypeScript, and OCaml.
ai_assisted_discovery_missions: Compare Go’s structural interfaces with Rust’s nominal traits.
How does TypeScript’s structural system handle type safety in a dynamic JS environment?
Explore the origins of Structural typing in the ML family.
